description: 알려진 엔티티 인스턴스 — 파이프라인 실행 시 자동으로 추가/업데이트된다.

properties.action_apr21: morning CNBC 'I don't want to' extend ceasefire; ~4pm ET Truth Social reversal: extends ceasefire indefinitely 'until such time as their proposal is submitted'; cited Iran 'seriously fractured' and Pakistan (Munir+Sharif) request; blockade maintained: 'continue the Blockade and, in all other respects, remain ready and able'

properties.action_apr22: IRGC seizes 3 ships in Hormuz (MSC-Francesca, Epaminondas, 3rd); Pezeshkian says 'open to deal' but accuses US of 'unconstructive signals'; dismisses Trump execution claims as 'false news'; IRGC escalates naval aggression despite ceasefire extension
2nd Washington talks with Lebanon confirmed Thursday Apr 24 (Huckabee joining); ceasefire fraying as Hezbollah continues attacks; continues joint war prep with US

seized MSC-Francesca (Panama, alleged Israel link) and Epaminondas (Liberia), fired on 3rd vessel in Hormuz; accused ships of AIS manipulation; UKMTO confirmed gunboat attacks off Oman coast; most aggressive naval action since blockade began

properties.status_apr22: IRGC seizes 3 ships, fires on all; Brent $101.91 (+3%) crossing $100 again; 'basically closed' (CNBC); dual blockade continues; Trump: Iran losing $500M/day; 6th consecutive day of major price swings

properties.day49_status: ceasefire day 10, Lebanon ceasefire day 1 (with violations), blockade day 5, Iran Hormuz 'open' but US blockade stays, 2nd round Monday Apr 21 confirmed, oil -11%

entities.name: UN Security Council Vote on Hormuz (Vetoed)

properties.targets: Tel Aviv, Haifa, Gulf oil facilities

entities.name: Tehran Severs Diplomatic Ties with US

properties.action_apr21: requested ceasefire extension from Trump (cited in Truth Social announcement); racing against time as Iran boycotts and Vance postpones
requested ceasefire extension from Trump alongside Sharif — cited by name in Trump Truth Social announcement; Pakistan's military-diplomatic dual track continues

properties.role: sole mediator in Iran war (WH confirmed)
properties.action_apr21: Munir + Sharif requested ceasefire extension from Trump — cited by Trump in extension announcement; racing against time as Iran boycotts talks

properties.action_apr17: claimed Hezbollah fight 'not over' despite ceasefire; publicly rebuked by Trump 'PROHIBITED from bombing'

properties.targets: Habshan UAE, Al Ruwais UAE, Sitrah Bahrain, Shuaiba Kuwait

entities.name: Iran Ballistic Missile Strikes on Israel (Apr 7)

properties.action_apr21: did NOT fly to Islamabad; trip 'postponed indefinitely' (Axios) as Iran boycotts 2nd round

properties.status_apr19: rift with IRGC deepens further; Vahidi identification as 'power behind the throne' confirms structural marginalization of diplomatic track

properties.action_apr21: adviser says: 'Trump's ceasefire extension means nothing, the losing side cannot dictate terms'

properties.action_apr22: continues firing on Israeli military sites day 2; ceasefire 'fraying'; both sides accuse other of violations; 200+ violations cited since ceasefire began

properties.status_apr22: ceasefire day 6, fraying; Hezbollah continues attacks; 2nd Washington talks Thursday Apr 24; Lebanon hopes for ceasefire extension beyond Apr 26 expiry

properties.context: Hours after Iran ceasefire declared, 'Operation Eternal Darkness', 'Black Wednesday'

properties.participants: US (Vance, Witkoff, Kushner), Iran (Araghchi, Ghalibaf ~70 people), Pakistan (host/mediator)
properties.sticking_points: nuclear (900 lbs enriched uranium), Hormuz control, frozen assets

properties.detail: Iran charges $1-2M per ship, Trump demands toll-free passage, 'serious disagreement' in Islamabad talks

properties.context: 40th day after Ali Khamenei death, declares 'new phase' in Hormuz management

properties.detail: $1/barrel oil toll in cryptocurrency

properties.vessels: USS Frank E. Peterson, USS Michael Murphy (Arleigh Burke-class)

properties.participants: Israel (Ambassador Yechiel Leiter), Lebanon (Ambassador Nada Mouawad), US (Secretary Marco Rubio, mediator)
properties.outcome: agreed to hold further talks at mutually agreed time and venue
properties.context: first direct talks since 1993, Hezbollah stepped up fire during talks

properties.items: Hormuz sovereignty, frozen assets ($6B), war reparations, region-wide ceasefire

properties.context: Iran demands release as negotiation precondition

properties.detail: Effective immediately, complete blockade of all ships entering/leaving Hormuz; interdict vessels that paid Iran tolls; begin mine clearing; 50% tariff on Iran-assisting countries

entities.name: Enriched Uranium Issue (900 lbs at Isfahan)

properties.detail: US demanded Iran hand over or destroy ~900 lbs (400kg) of near-bomb-grade enriched uranium at Isfahan; Iran refused; Trump: 'the only point that really mattered, NUCLEAR, was not'

properties.action: urged continued ceasefire, said Pakistan will facilitate new round of dialogue

properties.statement: gaps remain on several major issues

properties.detail: Trump threatens 50% tariff on any country assisting Iran, including potentially China

properties.scope: all maritime traffic to/from Iranian ports
properties.first_vessel: sanctioned tanker Elpis passed through; Ostria turned back

properties.detail: Araghchi claims US and Iran were 'inches away' from signing a memorandum of understanding. Clearest signal both sides came closer to formal agreement than publicly acknowledged. Blamed US 'maximalism, shifting goalposts, and blockade'.
US proposed Iran accept 20-year moratorium on all uranium enrichment and remove all HEU from country. Iran counter-offered 'monitored process of down-blending' existing stockpile.

properties.statement: As for the outcomes of this negotiation between Lebanon and the Israeli enemy, we are not interested in or concerned with them at all.
Called on Lebanon to cancel April 15 Washington meeting with Israel. Described talks as pointless.

properties.detail: Wafiq Safa: won't abide by any agreements. Naim Qassem: cancel April 15 talks. Over 2000 killed and 1M displaced in Lebanon since war. Hezbollah entered war firing missiles into Israel from March 2.
Hormuz faces dual blockade: Iran blocks free passage (tolls/mines) while US blocks Iran-bound traffic. Pre-war 135 ships/day → 4-10 ships/day. Near-total maritime paralysis.
UN FAO warns global food crisis could develop if normal Hormuz traffic doesn't resume. Substantial proportions of world farming products choked off. Largest oil supply disruption in history.

properties.action: mediated Israel-Lebanon Washington talks (Apr 14)

properties.statement: 'Highly probable' that peace talks will restart

properties.note: Physical crude still near $150/bbl (IEA), futures-physical disconnect widens

properties.assessment: largest energy security threat in history
properties.refinery_cuts: ME/Asia refineries cut 6 mb/d to 77.2 mb/d

properties.total_transits: at least 9 commercial vessels since Monday
properties.centcom_claim: no ships bound for Iran ports crossed blockade line

properties.detail: Trump told NY Post 'something could be happening over next two days'. NBC: talks could resume this week, possibly April 16. UN SG Guterres: 'highly probable'. Iran counter-offered 3-5 year moratorium vs US 20-year demand.
US waiver allowing countries to buy Russian crude expired, removing key supply source especially for India
Lebanese government and Hezbollah clash over Israel negotiations, sectarian tensions re-emerging

properties.action: issued first formal Red Sea blockade threat (Apr 15)
accompanied Munir to Tehran for mediation (Apr 15)

properties.detail: Trump told Fox Business war is 'very close to over' and stock market 'is going to boom'. Told NY Post talks could resume 'within two days' in Pakistan. Strongest language yet on war ending.
properties.context: Day 47 of conflict, day 8 of ceasefire, 6 days to expiry

properties.detail: CENTCOM declares blockade 'fully implemented' in less than 36 hours, completely halting Iran's economic sea trade. 10,000+ US personnel deployed. 6 merchant ships turned back. No ships made it past.

Khatam al-Anbiya Central HQ Commander Ali Abdollahi threatens to block ALL trade through Persian Gulf, Gulf of Oman, AND Red Sea unless US lifts blockade. First formal Red Sea blockade threat from Iran.
properties.significance: Qualitative escalation — extends conflict zone from Hormuz to global shipping lanes

properties.detail: Pakistan FM Asim Munir arrives in Tehran with Interior Min Mohsin Naqvi, carrying US message. Met FM Araghchi. Aim: persuade Iran to return to negotiating table for 2nd round.
properties.significance: Munir has rapport with Trump ('favorite field marshal') and knows IRGC leadership from intel service days

properties.detail: Iran government estimates total war losses. Demands reparations from US, Israel, and 5 Arab states (Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, UAE, Jordan) accused of allowing territory for attacks.
First intraday record since war began. S&P up 9.8% since Mar 30. Nasdaq +2%, Dow +0.7%. Market erased all war-related losses.
properties.driver: Trump 'war close to over' comments, 2nd round talks expectations

properties.trend: stable after previous day's -8% plunge, slight uptick
properties.context: Physical crude still near $150/bbl, futures-physical disconnect persists

properties.targets: US, Israel, Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, UAE, Jordan
properties.basis: territory used for launching attacks on Iran

properties.detail: Iran's first formal threat to extend blockade beyond Hormuz to Red Sea and Gulf of Oman. Qualitative escalation from regional to global shipping disruption.

properties.statement: blockade 'as long as it takes', US 'maximally postured' to resume military operations, 'if Iran chooses poorly, bombs on infrastructure'

properties.role: Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff
properties.statement: blockade applies to ALL ships regardless of nationality, US will use force if ships don't comply, 13 ships turned back, contraband includes weapons/nuclear materials/oil/metals
ceasefire extension 'not true at this moment', Pakistan is 'the only mediator', 2nd round talks 'very likely'

properties.action: agreed to 10-day ceasefire with Israel, spoke with Trump, invited to White House

properties.statement: 'We will be respecting the ceasefire' on condition Israel stops all attacks and assassinations

properties.detail: 10-day ceasefire as Israeli 'gesture of goodwill'. May extend if progress demonstrated. Trump invites both leaders to White House. First ceasefire since 1983. Israel keeps 'security strip' in south Lebanon.
properties.hezbollah_stance: conditional acceptance via Moussawi — if Israel stops all attacks

properties.detail: Defense Secretary Hegseth: blockade 'as long as it takes'. US 'locked and loaded' for attacks on Iran energy facilities. 'If Iran chooses poorly, blockade AND bombs on infrastructure.' US 'maximally postured' to resume military operations.

Gen. Dan Caine: US military can intercept any Iran-linked ship worldwide, broadening beyond Hormuz. Contraband: weapons, nuclear materials, oil, metals, electronics. 13 ships turned back (up from 6). No boarding yet. Pacific operations targeting pre-blockade departures.

Field Marshal Munir visits Khatam al-Anbiya Central HQ. Received by Abdollahi. Iranian official says visit narrowed differences on nuclear/Hormuz/reparations. Pakistani sources report 'major breakthrough' on nuclear programme. Munir engaged both civil and military leadership.
properties.significance: Key mediator engaging Iran's military establishment directly

properties.detail: PM Sharif met Saudi Crown Prince MBS, discussed mediation and regional stability. Arrived Doha Apr 16. Met Qatar Minister. 4-day tour to align regional support for Hormuz reopening and Iran talks.
Oil dips below $100. Stocks rally on Lebanon ceasefire and Iran deal optimism. Physical crude still near $150.

White House formally identifies Pakistan as 'the only mediator' in US-Iran negotiations. Karoline Leavitt: 'many countries offered help but president wants to streamline through Pakistanis'. Dual track: Munir in Tehran (military), Sharif in Gulf (political).

Pakistani sources report 'major breakthrough' on Iran nuclear programme after Munir's Tehran visit. Iranian official says gaps narrowed on nuclear issue among 3 unresolved areas (nuclear, Hormuz, reparations). US 20-year vs Iran 3-5 year moratorium gap may be narrowing.

Araghchi: Hormuz 'completely open' for commercial vessels during ceasefire. Conditions: commercial only, no hostile country cargo, designated routes via Larak island, coordinated with Iran Ports and Maritime Organisation. Duration tied to ceasefire period.
properties.trump_response: Thanks but US blockade 'will remain in full force' until peace deal

properties.detail: IRGC-affiliated Fars News citing SNSC source: Iran will close Hormuz again if US blockade not lifted. IRGC considers US blockade ceasefire violation. Contradicts Araghchi's opening — reveals hardliner/pragmatist tension within Iran.
CNN: Iranian sources confirm next round Monday in Pakistan. Both delegations arriving over weekend. Trump: expects deal 'in coming days'. Coincides with ceasefire expiry.
properties.status_apr21: effectively collapsed — Iran boycotts ('waste of time'), Vance did not fly ('postponed indefinitely'), replaced by indefinite ceasefire extension

properties.detail: Lebanese army reports Israeli shelling of southern villages. Machinegun/artillery at ambulance team in Kunin (Nabatieh). 1 killed, 2 wounded in Kounine (motorcycle struck). Israeli military warns residents north of Litani.
After Netanyahu said Hezbollah fight 'not over', Trump publicly stated Israel 'PROHIBITED' from bombing Lebanon during ceasefire. Rare public rebuke of allied leader.
properties.significance: US enforcement of Lebanon ceasefire against own ally; signals Trump prioritizes deal over Israeli preferences

properties.note: Biggest single-day drop since war began. WTI lowest since Mar 10. S&P 500 +1.2% (weekly +4.5%), Nasdaq +1.5% (weekly +6.8%), both at new record highs.

properties.detail: Lebanese civilians celebrate first quiet day since March 2 after 6+ weeks of war. 2,196 killed, 1M+ displaced in Lebanon.
Sharif arrives last leg of Saudi-Qatar-Turkey tour. Bilateral with Erdogan. FMs of Turkey, Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Egypt meet on sidelines to discuss war and Hormuz blockade.

Pakistan-proposed: 4 countries + IAEA monitor Iran nuclear programme. Iran agreed in principle, conveyed acceptance to Munir. US not yet formally responded. Options for 440kg enriched uranium: send abroad to third party or downblend to 3%.

Iran's 'opening' has 3 conditions: (1) commercial vessels only, (2) cargo/ships not linked to hostile countries, (3) designated routes via Larak island (Iran's side). Only handful of ships actually transit — shipowners wary. Duration tied to ceasefire.

properties.statement: 'No negotiating with clenched fists'; Turkey working to extend ceasefire and maintain talks

properties.detail: IRGC declares Hormuz 'returned to previous state' under strict military control, reversing Araghchi's opening within 24 hours. Cited continued US blockade as ceasefire violation. IRGC warned 'no vessel should make any movement from anchorage' and approaching Hormuz 'will be considered cooperation with the enemy'.

entities.name: IRGC Fires on Ships in Hormuz (Apr 18)

properties.detail: Iranian gunboats fired on at least 2-3 commercial vessels including Indian-flagged VLCC Sanmar Herald despite prior clearance to transit. UKMTO confirmed attacks by IRGC fast boats. At least one ship hit with some damage, no injuries. India summoned Iran ambassador.
properties.vessels: VLCC Sanmar Herald (Indian-flagged), at least 1 other Indian ship

properties.detail: Axios/CNN scoop: US considering unfreezing $20B in Iranian assets in exchange for surrender of enriched uranium stockpile. 3-page MoU under discussion. Under compromise, some HEU shipped to third country, some down-blended in Iran under international monitoring. Iran has asked for sanctions relief north of $20B.
Secretary of State Rubio urged European countries to quickly reimpose sanctions on Iran, warning Iran violating agreement and nearing nuclear weapon capability.

India's foreign ministry summoned Iran's ambassador over 'serious incident' of firing on two India-flagged merchant ships in Hormuz, especially after Iran earlier let several India-bound ships through.
Israel 'shocked' by Trump's PROHIBITED warning, formally demands explanation from White House. Netanyahu government questions whether Lebanon is even included in the ceasefire.

Largely holding. Israeli bulldozers continue demolition and land-clearing in south. Shelling around Beit Lif, al-Qantara, Toul. Displaced Lebanese cautiously attempting to return. Israel maintains 'extensive security zone' to Syrian border.

IRGC-affiliated sources called Foreign Minister Araghchi an 'idiot' for opening Hormuz, then reversed his decision within 24 hours. Most dramatic public display of hardliner-pragmatist split in Iran's power structure during the war.

US offers $20B frozen assets release for Iran's enriched uranium. 3-page MoU. Options: HEU to third country or down-blend to 3% under monitoring. Supersedes/complements 20-year moratorium demand. Iran wants more than $20B.
Oil prices rose after Iran re-closed Hormuz, partially reversing Thursday's 11% crash. Market whipsawed by 24-hour open-close cycle.

properties.action_apr20: confirmed as controlling military junta; inserted Zolghadr into Islamabad delegation to monitor diplomats; blocking Pezeshkian; 'power behind the throne' confirmed by multiple outlets

properties.detail: USS Spruance fired on engine room of Iranian cargo ship 'Touska' after 6 hours of warnings. First US seizure of Iranian vessel since blockade began. Iran calls it 'armed piracy' and threatens military response.
IRNA: Iran won't participate in 2nd round due to 'excessive demands' and US blockade. But Trump sending Witkoff/Kushner to Islamabad anyway (Monday night). Vance skipping.
Trump accuses Iran of 'total violation' of ceasefire, threatens to destroy infrastructure. Linked to IRGC actions at Hormuz.
US global ship seizure operations expanding beyond Hormuz. Follow-on to Caine's Apr 16 global blockade expansion declaration. Multiple seizure operations worldwide targeting Iran-linked vessels.
Israel establishes 10km 'Yellow Line' military zone in southern Lebanon, 55 villages, razing homes 'like Gaza' during ceasefire day 3. De facto occupation expansion under ceasefire cover.

properties.note: Third major swing in 3 days (crash → rebound → surge)

properties.detail: Audio recording of Sanmar Herald crew: 'You gave me clearance' — then fired upon by IRGC. Second ship Jag Arnav also attacked. Evidence of IRGC arbitrary fire despite prior transit authorization.
Zero tanker transits on Sunday Apr 19 — first time in modern history that no tanker has passed through Hormuz. Complete paralysis of world's most critical energy chokepoint.
Trump threatens to destroy 'every single Power Plant and Bridge in Iran'. Linked to 'total violation' accusation. Most specific infrastructure targeting language since war began.
Ghalibaf: 'impossible for others to pass Hormuz while we cannot'. Acknowledges progress but says 'many gaps remain'. Partial alignment with IRGC line but with diplomatic reservation.

properties.action_apr22: says Tehran 'open to deal' but accuses US of 'unconstructive and contradictory signals'; cites 'deep historical mistrust'; blockade is main obstacle to 'genuine negotiations'; first moderate signal since IRGC/Ghalibaf 'waste of time'

properties.role: IRGC representative inserted into Iranian negotiating delegation
properties.action: inserted by Vahidi despite Ghalibaf/Araghchi protests; filed complaint that Araghchi exceeded mandate by showing flexibility on Axis of Resistance

properties.action_apr20: attended Trump emergency meeting on Iran
attended Trump emergency meeting on Iran

properties.detail: Trump reverses from 'highly unlikely' (Apr 20) and 'I don't want to' (morning CNBC Apr 21) to indefinite extension via Truth Social: 'until such time as their proposal is submitted'. Cited Iran 'seriously fractured' and Pakistan (Munir + Sharif) request. Blockade maintained: 'continue the Blockade and, in all other respects, remain ready and able.'
properties.significance: Averts Apr 22 ceasefire expiry and war resumption; but blockade stays, IRGC dismisses as 'ridiculous spectacle'

properties.detail: Axios: Vance did NOT fly to Islamabad; trip 'postponed indefinitely' as Iran boycotts 2nd round talks. Was scheduled to lead 300-member delegation for face-to-face 2nd round.
properties.significance: 2nd round of US-Iran talks effectively collapsed

entities.name: Iran Official Talk Refusal via Pakistani Intermediary (Apr 21)

properties.detail: Iran officially refuses talks through Pakistani intermediary: negotiations 'waste of time'. Three reasons: (1) ceasefire not covering Lebanon, (2) excessive demands, (3) naval blockade. Ghalibaf adviser: 'Trump's ceasefire extension means nothing, losing side cannot dictate terms.' FM: US blockade is 'act of war.'
properties.significance: Strongest rejection yet — escalation from Apr 19 'no plans' to Apr 21 formal refusal with stated reasons

entities.name: Hezbollah First Rocket Fire Since Ceasefire (Apr 21)

properties.detail: Hezbollah fires rockets at Rab al-Thalathine + drone toward Israel. First Hezbollah attack since Apr 16 ceasefire. Claims 220+ Israeli violations. Legal Agenda: 220 breaches Apr 17-19, 3 killed, 7 wounded.
properties.significance: Threshold crossing — transforms Lebanon ceasefire from 'violations' to 'active combat resumption'

properties.detail: Kan broadcaster: senior Israeli security official says Israel is 'jointly preparing with Washington for war's resumption'. Skepticism about Iran deal as driver.
properties.significance: First explicit report of joint war resumption planning during ceasefire period

properties.after_hours: WTI $88.60, Brent $94.89 on ceasefire extension news

properties.vessels: MSC-Francesca (Panama, alleged Israel link), Epaminondas (Liberia), 3rd vessel
properties.detail: IRGC fired on all 3 ships, seized 2 (MSC-Francesca and Epaminondas), redirected to Iranian coast. Accused of AIS manipulation and operating without authorization. UKMTO confirmed attacks. Hours after Trump ceasefire extension. Most aggressive IRGC naval action since blockade began.

Trump tells Fox News' Martha MacCallum: 'no time frame' for ending war; denies midterms driving decisions; says Iran losing $500M/day from blockade; 'THE BLOCKADE is absolutely destroying Iran'. Leavitt: 'no firm deadline' for Iranian proposal. US officials initially said 3-5 day window but Leavitt contradicted.

Iran President Pezeshkian says Tehran 'open to deal' but accuses US of 'unconstructive and contradictory signals'. Cites 'deep historical mistrust'. Blockade is main obstacle to 'genuine negotiations'. First moderate signal from Iran leadership since IRGC/Ghalibaf 'waste of time' rejection — reveals civil-military split.

properties.note: 6th consecutive day of major price swings. Brent re-crosses $100 threshold. Physical crude still much higher.
Equities and oil moving in opposite directions — stocks cheer ceasefire while oil fears supply disruption. Divergence is structurally significant.

properties.participants: Israel (Yechiel Leiter), Lebanon (Nada Moawad), US (Marco Rubio, Mike Huckabee, Michel Issa, Michael Needham)
properties.purpose: extend ceasefire beyond Apr 26 expiry, build on Apr 14 first round
properties.context: ceasefire fraying with Hezbollah attacks, 10-day truce expires Sunday

properties.action_apr22: joining US delegation for 2nd round Israel-Lebanon Washington talks Thursday Apr 24

properties.detail: Trump orders US Navy to 'shoot and kill' any boat laying mines in Hormuz. 'No hesitation.' Tripled mine-sweeping. Claims 'total control' of Hormuz, 'Sealed up Tight.' Iran's navy 'all 159 ships at bottom of the sea.'

Trump announces 3-week extension after 2nd round WH talks. Was set to expire Apr 26. Rubio leads with Huckabee, Needham, Issa. Lebanon asks for end to IDF demolitions, withdrawal, prisoner release. Netanyahu and Aoun invited to WH.
properties.participants: Israel (Leiter), Lebanon (Moawad), US (Rubio, Huckabee, Needham, Issa)

properties.detail: Israeli Channel 12 reports Ghalibaf resigned from negotiating team due to IRGC interference. Iran denies. Oil spiked to $107. Peace deal market drops to 9% YES.

properties.cause: shoot-to-kill order + Ghalibaf report + Hormuz disruption

properties.significance: IRGC fast-attack boat staging area near Hormuz; 33 boats detected by Sentinel-2 satellite Apr 22

properties.action_apr23: met PM Sharif to discuss peace efforts

properties.role: Potential replacement for Ghalibaf as negotiation lead
properties.note: Hardliner, former nuclear negotiator (2007-2013), former SNSC secretary. If confirmed, signals Iran negotiation stance hardening.

properties.statement: 'The ceasefire is meaningless in light of Israel's insistence on hostile acts, including assassinations, shelling, and gunfire'

properties.detail: 60-day deadline for congressional authorization of Iran war. Trump notified Congress Mar 2, deadline May 1. Must seek approval or certify 30-day withdrawal extension. Senate defeated WPR 5 times (52-47 party-line). Legal debate on whether ceasefire pauses the clock.

Hegseth: 'shoot to destroy' any ship laying mines, Iran navy 'gang of pirates with a flag,' blockade 'as long as it takes.' Criticized Europe/Asia for 'freeriding' on US Hormuz action. Gen. Caine: blockade fully operational. Joint press briefing at Pentagon.

Iranian FM Araghchi arrives Islamabad Friday night with small delegation. IRNA says visit is 'bilateral' (not for US meeting). Expected to go to Muscat then Moscow. Possible US meeting Monday if Araghchi stays. Represents de facto reversal of Iran's Apr 19 official talk refusal.

White House confirms Witkoff and Kushner heading to Pakistan Saturday morning for direct talks with Iranian counterparts, 'intermediated by Pakistanis.' Vance 'on standby' but not attending. Leavitt: 'move the ball forward towards a deal.' Iran denies direct meeting planned.
properties.participants: Steve Witkoff, Jared Kushner, Karoline Leavitt

properties.driver: Hegseth 'as long as it takes,' Ghalibaf resignation, Hormuz deadlock

properties.detail: Israel killed 6 Hezbollah fighters in exchange of fire in Bint Jbeil area. 2 killed in IDF airstrike on Touline (southern Lebanon). Hezbollah fired rockets at Shtula settlement. All within 24 hours of 3-week extension announcement. Hezbollah lawmaker Ali Fayyad called extension 'meaningless.'

Senate Republicans defeated bipartisan Iran War Powers Resolution for 5th time, 52-47 party-line vote. Failed to curb Trump's authority to conduct military operations against Iran. 60-day WPR deadline is May 1.

Trump abruptly cancelled Witkoff/Kushner trip to Pakistan for 2nd round Iran talks. Blamed Iran 'infighting' and said meeting delayed to Tuesday. 'We have all the cards.' 'Too much time wasted on traveling.' After cancellation Iran sent 'much better' paper within 10 minutes. Araghchi had already departed Islamabad for Oman.

entities.name: Netanyahu Orders Forceful Strikes on Hezbollah (Apr 25)

properties.detail: Netanyahu ordered IDF to strike Hezbollah targets in Lebanon 'with force' after Hezbollah fired 2 projectiles (1 intercepted, 1 open area) + explosive drones at IDF troops south of Forward Defense Line. Triggered sirens in Manara, Margaliot, Misgav Am. 2nd Hezbollah ceasefire violation. Israel told US to pressure Lebanon or risk ceasefire collapse.
properties.casualties_day9: 4 killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon

properties.detail: Iran FM Araghchi departed Islamabad Saturday afternoon after meeting Munir and Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi. Delivered Tehran's list of demands for ending war to Pakistani officials. Traveled to Muscat, then planned Moscow visit. 'No direct talks with US' maintained.

Iran President Pezeshkian said Tehran will not enter 'forced negotiations' while facing pressure and threats. Demanded Washington first remove 'operational obstacles, including the blockade' before talks. Reversal from Apr 22 'open to deal' signal. Reflects IRGC influence over presidential messaging.

Trump: 'They gave us a paper that should have been better and interestingly the minute I cancelled it, within 10 minutes, we got a new paper that was much better…they offered a lot but not enough.' Reiterated Iran cannot get nuclear weapon. Suggested future talks by telephone.
properties.significance: Cancellation as negotiation tactic; Iran responsive

properties.education: CalTech BS 2017, Cal State MS CS
properties.action: stormed WHCD security checkpoint with shotgun/handgun/knives Apr 25, targeted Trump officials, called self 'friendly federal assassin'

properties.detail: Cole Allen storms security checkpoint, exchanges fire with law enforcement. Trump rushed out by Secret Service. Allen in custody. Federal charges filed. Trump doubts Iran war connection, says won't deter from 'winning war'.

properties.action: met Araghchi in Muscat Apr 26, discussed Hormuz and Iran mediation, rejected Hormuz toll proposal
will meet Araghchi Monday Apr 27 in St. Petersburg, Moscow as key diplomatic backer of Tehran

properties.location: Southern Lebanon, north of Litani River
properties.detail: Israel orders residents of 7 towns beyond buffer zone to evacuate. Towns outside 10km security zone. Major ceasefire escalation — de facto occupation expansion.

properties.status: killed 2026-04-26 by Hezbollah drone in southern Lebanon
properties.significance: 3rd soldier killed during ceasefire, 1st in direct Hezbollah attack

entities.name: Lebanon Day 10: Fooks Killed + IDF 14 Killed Retaliatory Strikes (Apr 26)

properties.detail: Hezbollah drone killed Fooks + 6 wounded. IDF retaliatory strikes kill 14 (incl. 2 women, 2 children), wound 37. Deadliest day since ceasefire began. Israel issues forced evacuation orders for 7 towns. Home Front Command limits gatherings in northern Israel.

properties.action: called Trump Apr 26 re Hormuz shipping, co-chairs 50-nation Maritime Freedom of Navigation Initiative with Macron

entities.name: Starmer-Macron Maritime Freedom of Navigation Initiative

properties.detail: 50+ country initiative co-chaired by UK PM Starmer and French President Macron to restore freedom of navigation in Strait of Hormuz once hostilities cease. 'Practical plan' discussed with Trump Apr 26.

properties.action_apr27: rejected Iran Hormuz proposal as 'unacceptable': 'Iranians don't get to decide who uses international waterways and how much you pay'. Called Iran's offer a system of 'coordinate with Iran, get our permission, or we'll blow you up'

spoke in Marsberg: US 'humiliated' by Iran, 'no truly convincing strategy'. Iran 'clearly stronger than one thought'. IRGC 'obviously negotiating very skilfully'. Compared to past US military debacles. European frustration over Hormuz economic impact
present at Putin-Araghchi meeting in St. Petersburg — signals military intelligence-level engagement in Iran mediation
present at Putin-Araghchi meeting in St. Petersburg
present at Putin-Araghchi meeting in St. Petersburg

properties.detail: Iran proposes via Pakistani mediators: reopen Hormuz + end war first, nuclear talks deferred to later phase. Decouples nuclear from immediate crisis. Trump reviews with NSC. Rubio rejects as 'unacceptable' — Iran retaining control of international waterway. US position: nuclear must be on table.

properties.participants: Araghchi, Putin, Lavrov (FM), Ushakov (Kremlin adviser), Kostyukov (GRU chief)
properties.detail: Putin pledges support: 'we will do everything that serves your interests.' Confirms receiving message from Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei. Araghchi briefs on Pakistan-mediated peace process. Putin sees Iran fighting 'bravely for independence and sovereignty.' GRU presence signals military intelligence engagement.

IDF strikes ~20 Hezbollah sites: first Beqaa Valley strike in 3 weeks. Weapon depots, rocket launchers, storage facilities targeted. Follows 14 killed Apr 26 retaliation. Ceasefire Day 11 — most geographically expanded Israeli strikes since ceasefire began.

Iran's new negotiation approach: decouple nuclear issue from immediate Hormuz/blockade/war crisis. Solve maritime/blockade first, nuclear in later phase. Strategic shift from previous all-in-one package. US rejects: Rubio insists nuclear must remain on table. Represents Iran's attempt to remove the biggest obstacle to a deal.

properties.action_apr28: Loses UAE as member effective May 1; second exit after Qatar (2019); Saudi-led cartel influence weakened; Iran war caused 7.88M bbl/day supply loss
Slammed GCC response as 'weakest in history'; said containment of Iran 'failed miserably'; expected weak stance from Arab League but not GCC
Chaired GCC consultative summit in Jeddah — first in-person since war began

properties.participants: MBS (chair), Qatar Emir, Kuwait Crown Prince, Bahrain King, UAE FM
properties.significance: First in-person GCC meeting since war; discusses response to Iranian attacks; amid UAE OPEC exit and Gargash criticism

properties.action_apr28: $500M superyacht Nord (142m) transited blockaded Hormuz from Dubai to Muscat over weekend; one of few vessels to pass since conflict began; sanctioned by US/EU
Stated Iran's armed forces do not view war as fully over; military conditions still in place; IRGC and army 'fully prepared' for further attacks if provoked

properties.significance: Nearly 60 years of membership ending; plans to increase production 3.4M→5M bbl/day by 2027; second GCC exit after Qatar (2019); weakens Saudi-led cartel; driven by Gargash 'weakest response' frustration and Iran war realignment

properties.detail: Trump Truth Social: 'Iran has just informed us that they are in a State of Collapse. They want us to Open the Hormuz Strait ASAP as they try to figure out their leadership situation.' Unconfirmed by Tehran. Iran losing >$400M/day. Iran army spokesman directly contradicted: 'still in war situation'.

Guterres addressed UNSC calling for immediate/unimpeded Hormuz reopening. Warned of global food emergency. Dozens of countries joined call. 20% of global LNG/oil transit disrupted. Millions in Africa/South Asia at hunger risk.

Mordashov's $500M superyacht Nord (142m) departed Dubai Friday, arrived Muscat Sunday transiting blockaded Hormuz. One of few private vessels to pass. Raises questions about blockade enforcement consistency and Russia's privileged access.
Trump announces US military will escort neutral commercial ships out of Hormuz starting Monday. CENTCOM: guided-missile destroyers, 100+ aircraft, 15,000 service members. ~20,000 seafarers stranded.

properties.statement: Advancing Hormuz navigation legislation; Iran will never retreat from inherent rights

properties.detail: Iran parliament advancing legislation: Israeli ships banned, hostile countries denied, permits required, tolls charged, violators face seizure + 20% cargo confiscation

properties.role: US General, ceasefire monitoring committee head

properties.detail: Trump tells Kan News: not acceptable. Iran not paid big enough price. Warns strikes could resume. Witkoff: we're in conversation.
Iran FM spokesperson confirms US responded to 14-point proposal via Pakistan. Tehran reviewing. First formal response cycle.
Exceptional meeting on ceasefire monitoring. Both stress LAF support. Israel ordered 11-village displacement same day.

First direct military exchange since April 8 ceasefire. US destroyed 6 Iranian small boats. Iran launched cruise missiles, drones, small boats at US ships and commercial vessels. Iran claims 2 missiles hit US frigate (CENTCOM denies). 2 US-flagged ships successfully transited. Trump: 'blown off the face of the Earth' warning.

UAE intercepted 15 missiles + 4 drones from Iran. Fire at Fujairah oil zone. 3 Indian citizens moderately injured. First Iranian attack on UAE since April 8 ceasefire. Fujairah critical as ADCOP pipeline terminus bypassing Hormuz.

IRGC declared 'smart control' zone. Southern boundary: Mt Mobarak (Iran) to south of Fujairah (UAE). Western boundary: Qeshm Island to Umm Al Quwain. Only 9 vessels transited in 24 hours. Vessels violating protocols will be 'forcefully stopped.' Called Project Freedom 'piracy' and ceasefire violation.

properties.action: Rejected negotiations with Israel until war stops. Told An-Nahar: 'stopping the war before any political track.' Most senior Shiite politician in Lebanon.

properties.role: Iran parliament security/foreign policy committee chair
properties.action: Warned US interference in Hormuz maritime regime = ceasefire violation.
Confirmed Iran reviewing US counter-proposal to 14-point plan.

properties.significance: End of ADCOP pipeline, bypasses Hormuz Strait. Critical to UAE oil exports during Iran war.

properties.action_may4: Intercepted 15 missiles + 4 drones from Iran. Fire at Fujairah oil zone. Schools shifted to remote learning Tue-Fri.

properties.action: Said transit through Hormuz still not possible despite Project Freedom.

properties.reason: Trump cites 'Great Progress' toward 'Complete and Final Agreement' with Iran; blockade remains in full force

properties.mechanism: transit permit system for Hormuz passage
properties.related: IRGC Maritime Control Zone, Iran Navigation Law

properties.action_may05: meets Araghchi in Beijing, first since Feb 28 war

properties.description: Largest and busiest harbour in the Middle East, Dubai, UAE

properties.action_may05: condemns Iran UAE attacks as 'unjustified and unacceptable'
Iran needs to engage meaningfully in negotiations
'Strongly condemn' UAE attacks (3 Indian nationals injured)

properties.role: Iran diplomatic partner, 25-year cooperation agreement, UNSC veto power

properties.detail: One-page memorandum of understanding declaring end to conflict. 30-day negotiation window for nuclear moratorium (never seek weapon, no underground facilities, snap inspections), gradual sanctions lift, frozen funds release, Hormuz restrictions + US blockade gradually lifted. Negotiated via Witkoff/Kushner through Pakistan. White House expects Iran response 24-48h. 'No deal yet' — US official.

Truth Social: 'If they don't agree, the bombing starts, and it will be, sadly, at a much higher level and intensity than it was before.' Operation Epic Fury restart threat. Also states 'never a deadline' for Iran.

IRGC Navy announces 'safe, stable' transit resumption. Ships must use Iran-designated corridor only. 'Good level of compliance.' 'Decisive action' against deviators. Framed as 'aggressor threats neutralized.' Strait Authority transit pass in effect.

properties.role: Operations Commander, Radwan Force (Hezbollah elite unit)
properties.detail: Led Radwan Force since January 2024 (after Wissam al-Tawil killed). Killed in Israeli strike on Dahieh, Beirut on May 6 along with deputy.
First strike on Beirut since April 16 ceasefire. Netanyahu + Israel Katz ordered. Coordinated with US. Killed Malek Ballout (Radwan Force commander) and deputy. Major ceasefire breach.

Brent $101.27 (-8%), WTI $95.08 (-7%). Biggest single-day drop since war began. Intraday low $98.30. Driven by MoU proximity reports, IRGC transit announcement, Trump 'great progress'. Still ~43% above pre-war levels.

Wang Yi: 'immediate end to hostilities', 'prompt resumption of shipping through Hormuz.' Appreciates Iran's no-nuclear-weapon commitment. Affirms peaceful nuclear energy right. One week before Trump-Xi summit May 14-15.
US-China summit. Originally March, delayed by Iran war. Iran/Hormuz, Taiwan, trade on agenda. Unfinished war gives Xi upper hand. China pressed Iran ahead of summit.
Confirmed Iran reviewing US proposal: 'after summing up its points of view, Iran will convey its views to the Pakistani side.'
Co-ordered Beirut Dahieh strike with Netanyahu targeting Malek Ballout.

properties.role: Attacked by IRGC while transiting Hormuz May 7; CENTCOM confirmed all threats intercepted, no US assets struck
Attacked by IRGC while transiting Hormuz May 7
Attacked by IRGC while transiting Hormuz May 7

properties.detail: Iran claims US airstrikes hit civilian areas here on May 7
Iran claims US airstrikes hit civilian areas here on May 7
Iran claims US airstrikes hit civilian areas here on May 7
Said US-Iran peace deal expected 'soon'; Pakistan converting ceasefire into permanent resolution

properties.method: multiple missiles, drones, small boat attacks
properties.targets: USS Truxtun, USS Rafael Peralta, USS Mason
properties.result: no US assets struck, all threats intercepted by CENTCOM

properties.targets: missile/drone launch sites, C2 locations, ISR nodes
properties.framing_us: self-defense, does not seek escalation, ceasefire still in place
properties.framing_iran: ceasefire violation, civilian areas on Qeshm Island/Bandar Khamir/Sirik hit

properties.detail: Compromise between Iran's 5-year and US's 20-year proposals for uranium enrichment freeze; discussed as part of MoU framework

properties.agenda: Full Israeli withdrawal, borders, prisoners, displaced persons, reconstruction

properties.detail: Nuclear verification (IAEA specifics), ballistic missiles, proxy forces (Hezbollah/Hamas/Houthis), HEU 900lb disposal — excluded from 1-page MoU, deferred to 30-day follow-up negotiations

properties.status: disabled by US Navy F/A-18 precision munitions to smokestack, Gulf of Oman, May 8
disabled by US Navy F/A-18 precision munitions to smokestack, Gulf of Oman, May 8

properties.role: launched F/A-18 Super Hornet that disabled Sea Star III and Sevda

properties.description: CENTCOM disabled M/T Sea Star III and Sevda via F/A-18 precision strikes to smokestacks
properties.context: blockade enforcement against vessels attempting to reach Iranian port

properties.definition: Trump's characterization of US strikes on Iran as minor ('just a love tap') while maintaining ceasefire is holding; escalation management through rhetorical minimization

properties.action_may8: met Rubio to discuss Iran war, US-Italy relations
met Rubio to discuss Iran war, EU-US relations

properties.stance: leading global critic of Iran war; met Rubio for 45+ minutes; Trump had repeatedly attacked pontiff

properties.significance: largest oil spill since Iran war began (Day 70)

properties.status_may8: US leadership questioned; Germany preparing 460K-troop defense plan; 5,000 US troops withdrawn from Germany

properties.impact: economic collapse risk if blockade continues

properties.significance: record low

properties.role: Adviser to Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, former First VP under Raisi
properties.action_may9: compared Hormuz control to 'atomic bomb' capability; pledged not to 'forfeit the gains of this war'; vowed to change Hormuz legal regime through international law or unilaterally
threatened 'heavy assault against one of the American centers in the region' if Iran's oil tankers face further aggression
declared 'missiles and drones are locked onto the enemy' and 'we are awaiting the order to fire'

properties.description: 26 attacks claimed including 2 inside Israel — first cross-border claims since April 16-17 ceasefire. Targeted Nahariya and Meron military bases. Israeli military intercepted one rocket, rest fell in open areas.

properties.location: Lebanon (Beirut suburbs, al-Saksakieh, Nabatieh, Nahrain, Saadiyat, Haboush, Mefdoun)
properties.description: 3 Israeli drone strikes south of Beirut kill 4; southern Lebanon airstrikes kill 13+ including children. Al-Saksakieh: 7 killed including child, 15 wounded. Total 19+ killed on Day 23 of ceasefire.

UAE Defense Ministry: air defenses engaged 2 ballistic missiles and 3 drones from Iran. 3 people sustained moderate injuries. Third attack on UAE since ceasefire began. IRGC denies any operations against UAE.

Iranian Navy commandos seized Barbados-flagged tanker Ocean Koi (IMO 9255933, also Jin Li) in Gulf of Oman. US-sanctioned since Feb 2026. Claimed vessel was disrupting Iran's oil exports. Escorted to southern coast.

Iran's elevation of Hormuz control to nuclear-level strategic asset. Mokhber: 'capability that can affect the entire global economy with a single decision.' Signals Iran may permanently alter Hormuz governance and legal regime, moving it from temporary war tactic to permanent strategic posture.

properties.role: Iranian Parliament Foreign Policy and National Security Committee spokesman
properties.action_may9: called US MoU text 'more of an American wish list than a reality'

properties.description: FAO global food price index reaches 130.7 in April 2026, highest since February 2023. Third straight monthly increase. Vegetable oil prices especially elevated by Iran war energy costs. Iran accounts for 3.5% of global urea. Fertilizer prices +20%. FAO warns global prices could average 15-20% higher in H1 2026.

Iran war likely to dominate summit agenda, leaving less scope for tariffs/rare earths. Bessent confirms Iran will be topic. Trump seeks China's support for Iran agreement. Beijing is Iran's biggest oil buyer. Rescheduled from late March after Iran war began.
Barbados-flagged tanker, IMO 9255933, also known as Jin Li. US-sanctioned since Feb 2026. Seized by Iranian Navy commandos in Gulf of Oman for allegedly disrupting Iran's oil exports.

entities.name: Iran formal response to US MoU via Pakistan

properties.description: Iran delivers multi-page counterproposal to US peace plan via Pakistan mediators. Demands: OFAC sanctions lift within 30 days, end to naval blockade, gradual Hormuz reopening. Nuclear enrichment declared 'non-negotiable red line.' Proposes separate 30-day nuclear talks, partial uranium transfer to third country.
Trump via Truth Social: 'I have just read the response from Iran's so-called Representatives. I don't like it — TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE!' Accused Iran of 'playing games.'
First LNG vessel to transit Strait of Hormuz since war began Feb 28. Loaded at Ras Laffan, bound for Pakistan's Port Qasim. Iran approved under government-to-government deal with Islamabad.
World's largest LNG exporter. Dual role as mediator and energy supplier. Two earlier LNG tankers aborted April 6 crossing. First successful transit May 10.
properties.action_may23: Qatar returns to active mediation after stepping back due to Iranian strikes on Qatari LNG infrastructure. Negotiating team arrived in Tehran, coordinated with US.

entities.name: Iran nuclear counter-offer (uranium dilution + third country transfer)

properties.description: WSJ/Bloomberg: Iran offers to dilute enriched uranium and transfer portion to third country (not US). Halts enrichment for shorter period than 20 years. Rejects dismantling facilities. Demands return clause if US withdraws. Marks shift from previous 'non-negotiable' stance.

Officials confirm Iran dominates agenda. Focus: Chinese purchases of Iranian oil, possible arms supplies, Hormuz reopening. Originally planned for March, delayed by war. Unfinished Iran war gives Xi leverage.
Bloodiest day since ceasefire (April 16). IDF struck 85+ Hezbollah sites in 24 hours. Saksakiyeh (7 dead including child), Nabatieh, multiple southern towns. 3rd Washington talks due May 14-15.
Qatar's main LNG production and export hub.
Pakistan's deep-water port, destination of Al Kharaitiyat LNG shipment.
Hezbollah artillery attack target on Israeli positions, May 10.
Hezbollah drone attack target on Israel border, May 10.
Iran demands return of transferred uranium if US withdraws from agreement — novel condition reflecting JCPOA trauma.
Trump declares ceasefire 'on massive life support', calls Iran proposal 'garbage' — rhetoric escalation from 'TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE'
Iran president: 'We will never bow our heads before the enemy' — negotiations not surrender
Iran warns European nations against sending warships to Hormuz — 'refrain from moves that undermine your interests'

properties.details: 20 warships, 2 CSGs (Bush, Lincoln), 61 vessels redirected, 4 disabled, Ford CSG returning after 322 days

properties.description: 3rd meeting between Lebanese and Israeli representatives, Thu-Fri May 14-15
'I don't think about Americans' financial situation, not even a little bit'
3-year high, energy +40%, gasoline +28.4% YoY
Pentagon war cost, up from $25B; Harvard estimates $1T
Planned renamed operation to reset WPR 60-day clock
4M bbl/day depletion, severely undersupplied until October
5-day drill near Tehran, US invasion simulation
Golani Brigade weeklong raid north of Litani, 100+ targets destroyed
35-40% global helium blocked, TSMC/Samsung affected
Key ceasefire boundary in Lebanon
20km south of Beirut, drone attack site
3-year US-China bilateral framework agreed at Beijing summit

properties.deal: 200 737 jets to China (unconfirmed by China), stock -4%

properties.description: Boeing 200 jets, soybeans, oil/LNG — 'small deal', no joint communique
2nd extension since April 16, next talks June 2-3, 657+ killed since ceasefire
Parallel military delegation talks at Pentagon for Israel-Lebanon
Araghchi admits deadlock, postponed to later stages, Russia offer mentioned
CENTCOM chief: Iran 90% degraded, generation to rebuild navy, every Epic Fury objective met
Israel targets most senior Hamas military leader
US plan for ending Gaza war including Hamas disarmament

properties.casualties: 55 killed in 48 hours, 657+ since ceasefire, 2951 total

properties.description: Araghchi: 'all countries acknowledge Iran was the victor'
Trump invites Xi for September visit to US
Rubio officially declares Operation Epic Fury objectives met
Trump officially threatens Operation Sledgehammer fresh strikes after Xi summit
IDF launches first strikes since ceasefire extended, 9 villages evacuated, ~100 targets
Trump proposes 20-year nuclear enrichment moratorium from AF1

properties.quote: He speaks of olive branch but we see only barbed wire

properties.event: Free Iran Rally DC video address

properties.followup: Security cabinet convened, May 19 NSC military review

properties.statement: No enemy military equipment through Hormuz

properties.features: designated lanes, toll collection, Hormuz Safe insurance, crypto payment, Israel ban

entities.name: Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani

properties.action_may19: requested Trump to postpone planned Iran strike; joined MBS and MBZ in coordinated Gulf appeal for 2-3 day delay to allow 'serious negotiations'
joined Tamim and MBS in requesting Trump to postpone Iran strike; UAE simultaneously investigating Barakah nuclear plant drone attack

properties.action: Trump called off planned 'very major attack' on Iran at request of Gulf allies (Qatar, Saudi, UAE); ordered Hegseth/Caine to stand down but military to remain ready for 'full, large scale assault on a moment's notice'; Gulf leaders said 2-3 days of negotiations could produce 'very acceptable' deal

Iran delivered modified proposal to US via Pakistani mediators; enrichment remains sticking point; US demands 20-year moratorium + transfer of 400kg enriched to 60%; Iran rejects as 'no basis in reality'; Trump says 'not open to any proposal'

properties.action_may19: Tasnim (Iranian media) reported US proposed temporary waiver on oil sanctions until final agreement; not confirmed by Washington; key demand by Tehran for reopening Hormuz; report caused Brent to drop from $111 to ~$102
SNSC announced creation of PGSA to manage Hormuz passage clearances and toll collection; formalizes Iran's institutional grip on the waterway; signals permanent rather than temporary wartime control

deployed to Gibraltar in rare public disclosure of SSBN location; carries up to 20 Trident II D5 missiles; bypassed Spain's Rota base (Spain refused use for Iran operations); nuclear deterrence signal to Iran
USS Alaska SSBN docked at South Mole; Royal Marines security; used as alternative to Spain's Rota after Madrid refused base access for Iran operations

properties.action_may20: flipped on WPR vote after primary loss; joined Paul/Collins/Murkowski — 50-47 advance marks first time war powers resolution cleared procedural hurdle

properties.significance: first time WPR resolution advanced past procedural vote; bipartisan momentum building against undeclared war

properties.action_may20: identified as most active proxy in Gulf drone warfare; leader arrested by US; UAE confirms Barakah drones originated from Iraqi territory where KH operates

properties.role_may20: confirmed origin of Barakah drone attacks; operating base for Kata'ib Hezbollah and other Iran-backed militias conducting Gulf drone warfare

properties.significance: official attribution of Barakah attack origin to Iraqi territory; 6 more drones intercepted in 48 hours
properties.details: UAE confirms all Barakah drones originated from Iraqi territory, implicating Iraq-based militias; ongoing threat with additional intercepts

properties.significance: shift from military strikes to targeted legal/intelligence operations against Iran proxies; signals new counter-proxy strategy

properties.action_may20: WPR co-sponsor with Rand Paul; leads bipartisan war powers push; sponsors resolution that advances 50-47

properties.action_may21: Beijing summit with Putin (May 19-20), ~40 deals signed, Power of Siberia 2 'main parameters' agreed; Iran war disrupts half of China's oil imports
confirms 3 drones intercepted from Iraqi airspace, aligns with UAE Barakah Iraq-origin confirmation; majority of attacks on Saudi originated from Iraqi territory
authors House WPR resolution, 4th vote expected this week after Senate 50-47 advance; previous 3rd vote tied 212-212

entities.name: Mohammad Baqer Saad Dawood al Saadi

properties.action_may21: charged with involvement in 20 attacks/attempted attacks in US and Europe, plus synagogue terror plot against American Jews

properties.detail: 2,600km Russia-China gas pipeline (50 bcm/year), 'main parameters' agreed at Xi-Putin summit; accelerated by Iran war energy disruption disrupting China's maritime oil/LNG routes

properties.role: House Foreign Affairs Committee Ranking Member (D-NY)
properties.action: Introduced House WPR resolution; GOP cancelled vote 2 days running fearing passage
First tanker HMM Universal Winner transited Hormuz with 2M barrels crude, no tolls paid, after 4 rounds of diplomatic talks with Iran

properties.detail: First Korean tanker since war began, 2M barrels crude, no tolls paid, 4 rounds of diplomatic talks. Part of IRGC 26-vessel transit claim.

Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei orders 60% enriched uranium to remain strictly inside Iranian territory, rejecting Trump's central demand for HEU removal. Israeli officials told Reuters Trump assured Israel HEU would be sent abroad.

GOP leaders cancel House WPR vote for 2nd straight day — feared it would pass due to Republican absences. 3 GOP defectors: Barrett, Fitzpatrick, Massie. Postponed to early June after Memorial Day recess.
properties.participants: Gregory Meeks, Josh Gottheimer, House GOP leadership

properties.detail: IRGC Navy claims 26 vessels transited Hormuz in 24 hours under IRGC coordination, including Chinese and Korean tankers. Reverses previous 0-vessel transit days. IRGC asserts Hormuz traffic control.
Iran offers to downblend HEU domestically to 3.7% and 20% levels, rejects sending abroad. Consistent with Khamenei retention order. Counter-proposal to Trump's demand for HEU removal.

properties.action_may23: Announced support for 'clean' WPR resolution — 60-day War Powers Act deadline passed. Previously sole Democrat voting against.

properties.detail: Trump announced on social media that deal with Iran 'largely negotiated, subject to finalization.' Called leaders of Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar, Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt, Jordan, Bahrain, and separately Netanyahu. Described as 'Memorandum of Understanding pertaining to PEACE.' Said 50/50 to make 'good deal' or 'blow them to kingdom come.' Will decide Sunday.
properties.participants: US, Iran, Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar, Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt, Jordan, Bahrain, Israel

properties.detail: Framework agreement to end war: stop fighting all fronts (including Lebanon), reopen Hormuz (lift US blockade, free commercial traffic, no tolls), release $25B frozen assets, sanctions lifting 'explicitly in text.' 30-60 days for detailed negotiations. Nuclear issues excluded from current talks. Iran Fars: Hormuz stays under Iran management.
NYT: 3 senior Iranian officials confirmed deal would release $25B in frozen Iranian assets overseas. Evolved from earlier $20B cash-for-uranium framework.

properties.action_may23: Met Munir during Tehran visit Saturday

properties.detail: Axios exclusive: 60-day MoU structure — Hormuz opens toll-free, mines cleared, US lifts blockade + sanctions waivers, Iran commits to never pursue nukes, negotiate enrichment suspension + HEU removal, US forces stay, 'relief for performance.' Draft approved by Ghalibaf, Vance, Witkoff, Kushner, sent to leaders for final approval.
properties.status: draft approved, pending final leader sign-off

properties.role: US Senator, Chair of Senate Armed Services Committee
Former CIA Director / Secretary of State

properties.detail: Republican internal criticism of Trump's Iran deal. Key critics: Wicker ('disaster'), Graham ('major shift'), Cruz ('disastrous mistake'), Pompeo ('not America First'). Represents significant intra-party friction on foreign policy.

properties.action_may26: Traveled to Doha as part of high-level delegation (with Ghalibaf/Araghchi) to discuss frozen assets release ($6B in Qatar banks)

properties.focus: frozen assets ($6B), Hormuz reopening, HEU — MoU sticking points
properties.status: intense talks continuing; consensus on many topics but signing not imminent
properties.significance: First time Qatar replaces Pakistan as primary negotiation venue; central bank chief's presence signals frozen assets as key lever

properties.detail: Trump demands 6 Muslim-majority nations (Saudi Arabia, Pakistan, Qatar, Turkey, Egypt, Jordan) join Abraham Accords as part of Iran deal; called it 'mandatory'; regional leaders responded with silence on the call; Trump joked 'are you still there?'; Saudi Arabia maintains Palestinian statehood precondition; could complicate or derail MoU

House Republicans pulled War Powers Resolution vote they were about to lose; 8 GOP members absent; delayed to June after Memorial Day recess; Democrats call it 'cowardly'; follows Senate 50-47 WPR passage with 4 Republican defections (Paul/Collins/Murkowski/Cassidy)
properties.significance: Growing bipartisan pressure to end unauthorized war; first House WPR near-passage

properties.action_may26: Called GOP 'cowardly' for pulling WPR vote; said Democrats had the votes

properties.role: Emerging co-mediator alongside Pakistan; hosts $6B Iranian frozen assets; site of intense Iran-US proxy talks (May 25-26)

properties.context: Transferred from South Korea to Qatar in 2023 for release of 5 US citizens detained by Iran; Iran demands unfreezing as precondition to MoU; Hemmati dispatched to negotiate release; actual total frozen assets may be much larger ($150B+)

changelog.reason: 트럼프 'life support'/'garbage' 에스컬레이션(src-1023), 페제시키안 'never bow'(src-1024), 레바논 Day 25 51명 사살(src-1025), 이란 유럽 군함 경고(src-1026), UK/France 해군 파견 검토, 봉쇄 현황(20척·61척 전환), 3차 워싱턴 이-레 회담 예고

트럼프-시진핑 베이징 정상회담 개시(src-1053), 트럼프 '미국인 재정 무관' 발언(src-1054), CPI 4월 3.8% 3년 최고(src-1055), 전쟁 비용 $29B(src-1056), '슬레지해머 작전' 개명 검토(src-1057), IRGC 테헤란 군사훈련(src-1059), 중국 유조선 호르무즈 시험(src-1060), IEA 원유 재고 기록적 감소(src-1061), 레바논 Day 28 15명 사망(src-1062), IDF 리타니강 이북 진출(src-1063), 3차 워싱턴 회담 개시(src-1064), 호르무즈 반도체 공급망 타격(src-1065)

트럼프-시진핑 정상회담 마무리('전략적 안정' 프레임워크, '스몰딜'), 보잉 200대 합의(시장 기대 500대 미달), 이스라엘-레바논 휴전 45일 연장(3차 워싱턴 회담), 아라그치 핵 교착 공식 인정(농축우라늄 후순위), CENTCOM 쿠퍼 청문회(이란 90% 열화), 이스라엘 하마스 하다드 가자 타격, IDF 레바논 48시간 55명 사살(다간 중사 전사), 위안화후 호르무즈 성공적 통과, 아라그치 '전쟁 승리' 선언, 트럼프 시진핑 9월 방미 초청, '에픽 퓨리' 종료 공식 선언

properties.action: Trump officially threatens 'Operation Sledgehammer' fresh strikes after Xi summit fails to produce Iran breakthrough; 'we'll finish the job'; commandos for nuclear retrieval considered; strikes could begin next week

properties.action_may16: returns after 11-month record deployment (longest since Vietnam), participated in Iran war opening + Maduro capture, Presidential Unit Citation awarded, 5,000 sailors return

properties.action: first wave of airstrikes since 45-day extension, 9 villages evacuated (Qaaqaaiyet al-Snoubar et al), ~100 targets hit, 2 Hezbollah terrorists killed, 1 IDF soldier killed
proposes 20-year enrichment suspension with 'real guarantee' — shift from permanent halt demand; AF1 press gaggle departing China

properties.role: Supreme Leader advisor / former SNSC Secretary
properties.action_may16: responds to Trump: 'ready to sign if all sanctions lifted'; 'He speaks of olive branch, but we see only barbed wire'; demands quick removal of all financial sanctions

properties.role: former NATO Supreme Allied Commander / retired General
properties.action_may16: speaks at Free Iran rally DC, calls for regime change in Tehran

properties.location: Upper Senate Park, Capitol Hill, Washington DC
properties.action: thousands of Iranian diaspora demand regime change, call for accountability on executions of political prisoners

properties.action_may16: speaks via video at Free Iran rally DC

changelog.reason: 트럼프 이란 공습 보류(걸프 3국 요청: 타밈/MBS/MBZ), 이란 수정안 파키스탄 경유 전달(농축 교착 지속), 미국 임시 제재 유예 제안(타스님 보도), PGSA 호르무즈 관리기구 설립(SNSC 발표), USS Alaska 핵잠 지브롤터 배치(핵 시그널링), 유가 $111→$102 급반전

상원 WPR 7차 투표 50-47 통과(캐시디 공화당 이탈), UAE 바라카 드론 이라크 발원 확인(48시간 6대 추가 요격), 카타이브 헤즈볼라(이라크 기반 이란 프록시) 식별 및 지도자 미국 체포, 트럼프 '2-3일' 최후통첩, 루비오-왕이 호르무즈 통행료 공동 반대(미중 이례적 합의), 유가 Brent ~$110, 12-15년 핵 모라토리엄 착륙지대 부상
하메네이 HEU 보유 명령(핵 협상 최대 쟁점 공식화), 하원 GOP WPR 투표 2일 연속 취소(그레고리 믹스 등장), IRGC 26척 호르무즈 통과·한국 유조선 첫 통과(HMM 유니버설위너·200만bbl), 이란 국내 다운블렌드(3.7%/20%) 제안, 루비오 'good signs', 무니르 테헤란 방문. 스키마 변경 없음
트럼프 'largely negotiated' MoU 선언(9개국+네타냐후), '50/50' 딜/폭격(일요일 결정), NYT MoU $25B 확인, 바가에이 '프레임워크'(30-60일·핵 제외), Fars '현실 불일치', 카타르 공동 중재, 무니르 테헤란 완료(페제시키안/갈리바프), IRGC 35→25척, 골든 WPR 전환
MoU 60일 프레임워크 세부사항 공개(Axios), 초안 4자 승인(갈리바프/밴스/위트코프/쿠슈너), 트럼프 '서두르지 말라' 지시, CBS '이란 HEU 폐기 원칙 합의', 루비오 인도에서 '좋은 소식 곧', 공화당 '재앙' 비판(위커/그레이엄/크루즈/폼페이오), IDF 레바논 작전계획(이란 딜 우려). 스키마 변경 없음

이란 도하 회담(갈리바프/아라그치/헤마티 카타르 이동·동결자산 $6B 핵심 쟁점), 트럼프 아브라함 협정 정상화 '필수' 요구(6개 이슬람 국가·지역 지도자 침묵 반응), 하원 GOP WPR 투표 취소(통과 직전·6월 연기·제프리스 '비겁'), 유가 Brent $96.31(4월 이후 최저), IRGC 32척 호르무즈 통과, 이스라엘 레바논 3명 사살·10개 마을 퇴거·IDF 1명 헤즈볼라 드론 전사. 스키마 변경 없음
